perturbation.original_text: Unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Receiving Party and the Company with respect to a Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the Receiving Party nor the Company will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression by either of them or their Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Receiving Party and the Company with respect to a Merger has been executed and delivered, neither the Receiving Party nor the Company will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression by either of them or their Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Transaction" to "Merger" creates ambiguity because the parties may not be sure whether they are refering to a transaction, or a merger, and introduces an in-text contradiction. Throughout the document, it refers to the term transaction. It is in question if it is the same as a merger.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Receiving Party and the Company with respect to a Merger has been executed and delivered, neither the Receiving Party nor the Company will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression by either of them or their Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Transaction' to 'Merger' in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text because the Contradicted Text refers to 'Transaction', while the Changed Text refers to 'Merger'. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, this creates ambiguity because the parties may not be sure whether they are referring to a transaction or a merger, and introduces an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its Representatives not to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the possible Transaction or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its Representatives not to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the potential Partnership or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Transaction" with "Partnership" causes confusion. By inserting the term partership, it causes doubt about if it is the same as a transaction. The rest of the document refers to a transaction, which this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its Representatives not to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the potential Partnership or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Transaction" to "Partnership" in the Changed Text introduces a contradiction with the Contradicted Text and the Original Text. While the Contradicted Text includes both "potential Partnership" and "other transaction," the Changed Text focuses on "Partnership" while the rest of the document refers to "Transaction". This creates ambiguity and doubt about whether they are interchangeable, leading to a misaligned terminology in-text contradiction. The explanation highlights this uncertainty, which is a direct conflict within the document, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Acquisition, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Transaction" with "Acquisition" introduces confusion whether they are the same. The rest of the document refers to the transaction, which this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Acquisition, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Transaction' with 'Acquisition' in the definition of 'Standstill Period'. The explanation states that the rest of the document refers to 'Transaction'. Because of this, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear if 'Acquisition' and 'Transaction' are meant to be the same, as the rest of the document refers to 'transaction' and not 'acquisition'. The contradictory text is still in the same location as the changed text, which is still a contradiction.
